cinema.name: Innuendo Film Fest
cinema.description: Welcome to the Innuendo International Film Festival 2026!

works.title: Bimbat. Life Above the Earth
works.description: A small-town bar audience in the Urals, attending a stand-up lecture, is whisked 60 years into the past. On that day, a Soviet cosmonaut performs a historic spacewalk. Meanwhile, engineer David Bimbat pilots the first Soviet helium airship. The astronaut is remembered by all. Bimbat is forgotten. But he was the one entrusted by Tsiolkovsky’s secretary with reviving the great scientist’s ideas. How did he end up in a tank-building city? And why did he start building airships there? This docudrama unveils — for the first time — the rise and fall of a dreamer-inventor who flew himself, and spent his life teaching others to fly.

works.title: Cuore Blu
works.description:
Cuore Blu showcases the largest wave ever surfed in the Mediterranean while delving into Simone Macera's personal bond with the sea. The film also explores the diverse ways in which others, including local elders, relate to the sea, offering a mosaic of perspectives on this vital element.
Based in Livorno, Italy, Simone’s story is one shaped by the many interpretations of the sea. For some, it’s a space for sunbathing; for others, it’s about fishing, sailing, or surfing. Yet, despite these varied pursuits, the sea ultimately serves as a place of profound self-reconnection, a place where one can reconnect with their true self, away from the distractions of modern life.

works.title: DYNAMICS
works.description:
DYNAMICS (Frozen Movements) is an experimental, dreamy and surreal stop-motion film, created using everyday materials. The film offers a visual reflection on transformations that emerge through the intuitive blending of dissimilar elements. The result is a non-narrative art film where fantasy, form and texture merge into a sensory experience.
This short animation invites viewers to surrender to a world where logic gives way to atmosphere and intuition. The hypnotic soundscape enhances the avant-garde character of the film and leaves plenty of room for personal interpretation.

works.title: Eight of Wands
works.description: An actress leaves for a journey together with a film-maker, TS Eliot's The Waste Land and a deck of Tarots. The omens look gloomy but she attempts to defy them through the very film they are shooting, improvising day by day, and little by little the frames start playing the role of cards in a cinematic deck of Tarots. At the end the response will be a new one, perhaps even more sibilline, her own.

works.title: Ekelbrode
works.description: In this dark science fiction tale, Cadulbrockt faces his final night aboard a roaming prison wagon that crawls across a toxic wasteland. Once a soldier in a failed revolution, he now labors under the rule of the Aelsassi empire. When Malta, a brooding mycologist, confronts him in his cell, buried memories of war, guilt, and betrayal resurface. As secrets emerge and trust fractures, Cadulbrockt must decide whether to protect himself or risk everything for a fragile hope of reclamation.

works.title: Flying with Fins
works.description:
Paintings, performances, experiments, electronic music sounding in the space of two old houses in a small Italian town, heated conversations about contemporary art, touching meetings with the closest people and places in Bulgaria after 50 years of separation.
FLYING WITH FINS is a film about the constant search for meaning in art and life. Alzek Misheff, artist - rebel and experimenter, leads us in this philosophical and aesthetic journey through time, space and ideas.

works.title: Fragile Truth
works.description: Dr. Johan Faber, a respected psychiatrist, is drawn into a chilling mind game with his enigmatic patient, Thomas. What begins as routine therapy spirals into a battle of wits as Thomas exposes Faber’s deepest fears and buried secrets. As reality blurs, Faber questions whether Thomas is truly a patient or a manifestation of his unraveling psyche. In a harrowing climax, he confronts the terrifying truth—his greatest enemy may be his own mind. Was anything ever real, or is truth just an illusion?

works.title: Land of the Mustaches
works.description:
For the children and women of all wars, it never leaves. 
Land of the Mustaches is an autobiographical story of a young girl growing up in the Displaced Persons refugee camps with her mother and grandmother after World War Two left them starving and stateless, and her father's disappearance into Stalin's notorious Gulag labor camps in Siberia with only what he was wearing the day he was abducted—two hard paths that parallel each other until they finally merge into one story: The ultimate celebration of resilience and the human spirit.

works.title: NADO
works.description:
NADO is an intimate and powerful journey through the life and work of one of the greatest Italian sculptors of the twentieth century. A story of resistance and courage, a human parable of will and joy that spans a whole century.
Nado Canuti has faced the difficulties of life since he was young with tenacity and vision, transforming every obstacle into material to be sculpted. The story of a man who with three fingers
shaped his destiny.

works.title: NO
works.description: In the sterile corridors of power, a high-level diplomat is haunted by surreal glitches as he prepares to cast a decisive veto against a life-saving ceasefire. His internal struggle is contrasted with the desperate, fragile hope of a humanitarian doctor in a war-torn refugee camp, whose world hangs in the balance. The film climaxes in the silent, tense chamber where a single word spoken becomes an act of immense violence, with immediate and tragic consequences.

works.title: Prampolini-Menarini Express
works.description: A found footage / object film: the colorful 1960s in Italy, a joyful time, live-giving coating of born-again found images, re-animated, examined, reviewed in a past time, revisited.

works.title: Social Spheres
works.description: What happens when our online communities, our social spheres, consume too much of our time? This short animation considers that very question in a ludicrous, but beautiful, manner.

works.title: The Rites of Man
works.description:
Time-traveling anthropologists document the heartbreak of an aspiring entrepreneur and his discovery of an alien substance living inside him.
The Rites of Man is a work of poetic existential philosophy, inspired by the work of Byung-Chul Han, Simone Weil, Hannah Arendt, and Zygmunt Bauman. It plumbs questions of motivation and meaning in the 21st century: what forces drive our hustle for success in this technological world we’ve created.

works.title: Thoughts of Infinity
works.description:
Thoughts of Infinity is a feature-length documentary that invites audiences into the artistic and philosophical universe of James Howell, an artist whose lifelong pursuit centered on the “in-between” — the space where opposites dissolve and absolutes give way to nuance. Known for creating more than 400 works that explore subtle tonal transitions, Howell saw gray not as a single color but as a state of being, a continuum that reflects the fluidity of existence itself. His practice, precise yet meditative, asks us to pause, to perceive differently, and to encounter a world beyond polarization.
Blending evocative cinematography with archival material and reflective testimony, Thoughts of Infinity traces Howell’s journey through the voices of family, friends, and collaborators. Their memories not only illuminate the man behind the canvases but also shed light on the challenges he faced in an art world often driven by market value and spectacle — one that frequently overlooked his quiet, singular pursuit.
Moving between biography and meditation, the film gradually reveals itself much like Howell’s paintings: through patience, precision, and attention to detail. It weaves together the intimate and the universal, carrying viewers from the frenzy of everyday life into the stillness of an artist who discovered infinity within limitation. At once a portrait and a philosophical inquiry, Thoughts of Infinity reflects on how Howell’s dedication to the in-between continues to expand the way we see, think, and feel.

works.title: Waves Won't Stop
works.description: An experimental documentary in which the narrator evokes his childhood memories of Cyprus, and his connection to an indelible friendship defined by separation. Through vivid recollections of their lost bond, he explores themes of longing, belonging, and on the enduring pull of his homeland that shaped them both. "Waves Won’t Stop" is an intimate look on a long journey of return.

works.title: Wind's Lips
works.description: Through the corridors of a Parisian cinema, sensuality and memory intertwine, capturing the fragile intensity of fleeting moments.
